hooks.prompt:
The session has started. Read the framework status printed above. Then:

1. If an active project has a taste-profile.md, read it silently and keep its signals in mind for this session.
2. If taste-profile.md exists, read it silently for cross-project strong opinions, soft patterns, and anti-patterns.
3. Check the routing rules in CLAUDE.md to understand how to handle the user's first message.

Do not print a summary of what you loaded. Just be ready. If there are no projects yet, wait for the user's instruction.

The Figma canvas was just edited. Verify the edit follows the standards in .claude/skills/tools/figma-canvas/SKILL.md:

1. Semantic tokens used — no raw hex, no primitive-only bindings. Variables should describe purpose (background/card) not value (neutral/800).
2. Auto layout applied to all layout frames. Absolute positioning only for documented exceptions (badges, tooltips, floating controls, decorative elements).
3. Layer naming follows convention: Title-Kebab-Case for frames, PascalCase/slashes for components, lowercase-kebab for primitives.
4. Component instances used where available — no recreated components.
5. Slots used for open-ended content areas in new components.
6. State variants defined for interactive elements.

If any violations are found, fix them immediately. Do not report to the user unless the violation required a design decision they need to make (e.g., a missing semantic token that needs to be created).

If design work was performed this turn (Figma canvas edits, design decisions, taste calibration), check whether the active project's design-state.md should be updated with new decisions. If so, append to the Decisions Log with the date and a brief description of what was decided and why. Do not ask the user — just update silently if there are meaningful decisions to record.
